title: 'Pivotal' Q3 Sees Insurtech Root Achieves Net Income Profit for the First Time
text: Root Inc., the Columbus, Ohio-based insurance technology company established in 2015, has achieved a historic milestone by recording its first quarterly net income profit. The company reported third quarter net income of approximately $23 million, compared to a net loss of roughly $46 million during the same period last year. Year-to-date results also show improvement, with net income of nearly $9 million versus a $123.4 million loss in the prior year. CEO and co-founder Alex Timm characterized the achievement as transformative, stating: This quarter validates the strength of our business model, our technology, and our customer value proposition. Operational metrics demonstrate significant improvement across multiple indicators. Root's combined ratio improved substantially to 91.1 in Q3 2024 from 143.1 in Q3 2023. Net premiums earned increased by $179.3 million to reach $279.3 million, reflecting decreased cession of gross premiums to reinsurers. Policies in force grew 57 percent year-over-year to 407,313, with the partnership channel experiencing particularly strong growth at 131 percent in new writings. Timm noted that independent agents represent an important market segment, especially for customers with complex coverage needs. Root's expansion strategy includes technology integration partnerships, including a recent arrangement with Goosehead Insurance announced in August. The direct channel benefitted from reduced market competition in 2023, enabling the insurer to double its business portfolio in under twelve months. The company currently operates in 34 states and is actively pursuing nationwide expansion. Recent refinancing efforts reduced debt and cut interest expenses by approximately 50 percent.
